the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (0.4% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Crunch (it never builds Berserker's Axe, Salvation) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime and extended fights: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +14.1% on the burst combo at minute 15.

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Physical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 199 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +66% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Augmentation (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,292 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser (Physical-Def)
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 750 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 1% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 13% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 24% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 27% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 14% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 24% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

laneSteer.provenance: “Combo Crunch” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field offlane 46.9% over 2,573 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 19% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 24% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Basilisk: their burst combo hits 14% less hard, and this kit gives up 20% of its damage for it.
